per_question.answer_preview: Task::ready(): creates already-resolved Task<T>. Used when: (1) result immediately available (no async), (2) need to return Task from sync context. Example: if cache_hit { return Task::ready(cached_va

per_question.expected_preview: Anyhow's context methods add descriptive information to errors as they propagate. Found throughout Z
per_question.answer_preview: .context(msg: &str): eager context addition, msg evaluated immediately. .with_context(f: Fn() -> String): lazy context, f called only if Err. Difference: with_context avoids allocating context message

per_question.expected_preview: The bail! macro in Zed provides early error returns. Found in lmstudio.rs, vim/command.rs, agent_ui

per_question.expected_preview: In crates/client/src/telemetry.rs (lines 82-90), Zed implements event integrity verification using Z
per_question.answer_preview: ZED_CLIENT_CHECKSUM_SEED: constant seed for client telemetry validation. Events include checksum computed as: hash(event_data + seed). Server validates: recomputes hash, compares with event checksum.

per_question.answer_preview: Invalid regex patterns: validation on load, invalid patterns rejected (error logged, tool disabled). Why security: regex injection could bypass pattern matching. Validation: compile pattern on load, c

per_question.answer_preview: always_allow_tool_actions: global override allowing all tools. Does NOT override: (1) always_deny patterns (explicit block always enforced), (2) tool_disabled flags, (3) user capability restrictions.

per_question.answer_preview: disjoint selections: non-overlapping, properly ordered. pending selections: currently being modified (e.g., during drag selection). Guarantee: at any time, either (1) all selections disjoint + no pend

per_question.answer_preview: Invariant: transactions grouped only if within GROUPING_WINDOW (500ms by default). History tracks: last transaction timestamp. On new transaction: (1) check time since last, (2) if within window, grou

per_question.expected_preview: Editor
per_question.answer_preview: Editor is the main struct in the editor crate, representing an editor view/entity managing a buffer, display map, selections, and rendering.

per_question.expected_preview: gpui
per_question.answer_preview: GPUI is the UI framework crate in Zed, providing the layout, rendering, event handling, and component system for Zed's interface.

per_question.expected_preview: Workspace
per_question.answer_preview: Workspace is the main struct in the workspace crate, managing panes, items (tabs), project references, and overall editor layout.

per_question.expected_preview: MultiBuffer
per_question.answer_preview: MultiBuffer is the struct representing Zed's multi-buffer, enabling display of multiple buffers or excerpts as a single continuous text stream.

per_question.expected_preview: Project
per_question.answer_preview: Project is the main struct in the project crate, managing worktrees, buffers, LSP servers, git information, and project-wide state.

per_question.expected_preview: Fs
per_question.answer_preview: Fs is the trait representing a filesystem interface in Zed, providing file read/write, directory scanning, and metadata operations.

per_question.expected_preview: Buffer
per_question.answer_preview: Buffer is the struct representing a text buffer in Zed, storing text content, history, and managing edits/transactions.

per_question.expected_preview: ThemeRegistry
per_question.answer_preview: ThemeRegistry is the struct in the theme crate for managing themes, loading and switching between color themes.

per_question.expected_preview: Rope
per_question.answer_preview: Rope is the main struct in the rope crate, a text data structure implementing efficient text storage and manipulation operations.

per_question.expected_preview: LanguageRegistry
per_question.answer_preview: LanguageRegistry is the struct representing language registry in Zed, managing languages, parsers, and adapters.

per_question.expected_preview: SettingsStore
per_question.answer_preview: SettingsStore is the struct that handles settings in Zed, managing user settings, language-specific settings, and path-specific overrides.

per_question.expected_preview: BufferDiff
per_question.answer_preview: BufferDiff or DiffHunk represents buffer diff operations in Zed, storing diff hunks (added/removed/modified regions).

per_question.expected_preview: main
per_question.answer_preview: The main function is located in src/main.rs of the zed crate, as is standard for Rust binaries.

per_question.expected_preview: AppState
per_question.answer_preview: AppState in the collab crate represents the shared application state in the collaboration server, managing users, projects, and connections.

per_question.expected_preview: HighlightId
per_question.answer_preview: HighlightId is the struct representing an individual highlight ID in Zed, used to apply syntax highlighting to text ranges.
